title: Hermes Agent
voiceScript: Most AI forgets everything the second you close the chat. This one rewrites itself to get smarter. You re-explain the same context, fix the same bug, repeat the same task. Every session starts from zero. So I started running Hermes, an open source agent that writes its own skills. Solve a hard problem once, and it never relearns it. The setup is wild. It unifies over 20 messaging platforms, runs on a five dollar server, and curates its own skills every seven days. Your AI should compound like an employee, not reset like a tool. If your agents still feel like chatbots, this one is worth a look. Follow for more.

source: LinkedIn/overview post on Hermes Agent by Nous Research (open-source, MIT, early 2026). Self-improving autonomous agent: closed learning loop that writes its own reusable skills (procedural memory), FTS5 cross-session persistent memory, universal deployment (local terminal, Docker, $5 VPS, Modal/Daytona serverless), multi-platform gateway across 20+ messaging apps (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, WeChat, Teams), Nous Tool Gateway (Firecrawl search, FAL/FLUX image, OpenAI TTS, Browser Use). Releases: v0.12 Curator (autonomous skill grading/pruning on a 7-day cycle), v0.13 multi-agent Kanban boards, v0.15 -76% codebase + promptware defense, v0.16 native desktop app. Pairs with Nous Hermes 3 (Llama 3.1 8B/70B/405B). Positioned as a no-code alternative to n8n.
